We believe this autoregressive formulation represents a new paradigm for avatar generation, and we will release our code inspire future research.

ru.title: Автороgressивное поколение фотореалистичных 4D аватаров через облака точек
ru.desc: AvatarPointillist представляет собой инновационный фреймворк для создания динамических 4D аватаров на основе 3D Gaussian Splatting. В основе метода лежит декодер-only трансформер, который автореgressивно генерирует облако точек с адаптивной плотностью, динамически подстраивая количество точек под сложность объекта. Одновременно с генерацией точек модель предсказывает информацию привязки для каждой точки, что позволяет реалистично анимировать аватар. Дополнительный Gaussian декодер преобразует сгенерированные точки в полные атрибуты Гауссианов, обеспечивая высокое качество и фотореалистичность итогового результата.

en.title: Revolutionizing Avatar Creation with Dynamic 4D Gaussian Models
en.desc: AvatarPointillist is a new method for creating dynamic 4D avatars from a single image using a decoder-only Transformer. This approach generates point clouds autoregressively, meaning it builds the 3D representation step by step, adjusting the density of points based on the complexity of the subject. The model also predicts binding information for each point, which helps in creating realistic animations. Our experiments show that this method produces high-quality, photorealistic avatars and sets a new standard for avatar generation in machine learning.

zh.title: 动态生成高质量4D高斯头像的创新方法
zh.desc: AvatarPointillist 是一个新颖的框架，可以从单一的肖像图像生成动态的 4D 高斯头像。该方法的核心是一个仅使用解码器的 Transformer，它自回归地生成用于 3D 高斯点云的点。通过这种顺序生成的方法，可以根据对象的复杂性动态调整点的密度和数量。同时，模型还预测每个点的绑定信息，从而实现逼真的动画效果。实验结果表明，AvatarPointillist 能够生成高质量、逼真且可控的头像，代表了头像生成的新范式。

papers.title: Synthetic Sandbox for Training Machine Learning Engineering Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract A multi-agent framework called SandMLE is introduced that generates synthetic machine learning engineering environments from limited seed tasks, enabling efficient on-policy reinforcement learning by reducing dataset sizes and computational costs while maintaining problem complexity.  AI-generated summary As large language model agents advance beyond software engineering (SWE) tasks toward machine learning engineering (MLE), verifying agent behavior becomes orders of magnitude more expensive: while SWE tasks can be verified via fast-executing unit tests, MLE verification requires running full ML pipelines -- data preprocessing, model training, and metric evaluation -- on large datasets at each rollout step, rendering trajectory-wise on-policy reinforcement learning (RL) prohibitively slow. Existing approaches retreat to supervised fine-tuning (SFT) or offline proxy rewards, sacrificing the exploration and generalization benefits of on-policy RL. We observe that sandbox data size is the primary source of this bottleneck. Based on this insight, we introduce SandMLE, a multi-agent framework that generates diverse, verifiable synthetic MLE environments from a small number of seed tasks, preserving the structural and technical complexity of real-world problems while constraining datasets to micro-scale (each task is paired with only 50-200 training samples). Through extensive experiments, we show that SandMLE reduces execution time by over 13 times, enabling large-scale, on-policy trajectory-wise RL for the first time in the MLE domain. On MLE-bench-lite, SandMLE yields significant gains over SFT baselines across Qwen3-8B, 14B, and 30B-A3B, with relative medal rate improvements ranging from 20.3% to 66.9%. Furthermore, the trained policy generalizes across unseen agentic scaffolds, achieving up to 32.4% better HumanRank score on MLE-Dojo.

ru.title: Синтетические окружения для эффективного обучения агентов машинного обучения
ru.desc: В статье представлена SandMLE — фреймворк на основе мультиагентной системы, который генерирует синтетические окружения для машинного обучения из небольшого количества исходных задач. Основная идея заключается в том, что верификация агентов в задачах машинного обучения требует запуска полных ML-пайплайнов, что значительно дороже, чем проверка задач по инженерии программного обеспечения. SandMLE решает эту проблему, генерируя разнообразные синтетические среды с малыми датасетами (50-200 примеров), которые сохраняют структурную сложность реальных задач. Благодаря этому подходу удалось ускорить выполнение в 13 раз и применить on-policy reinforcement learning в области машинного обучения, достигнув улучшений на 20-67% по сравнению с базовыми методами supervised fine-tuning.

en.title: Revolutionizing MLE with Synthetic Environments for Efficient RL
en.desc: The paper presents SandMLE, a multi-agent framework designed to create synthetic machine learning engineering (MLE) environments from a limited number of seed tasks. This approach allows for efficient on-policy reinforcement learning (RL) by significantly reducing the size of datasets and computational costs while still maintaining the complexity of real-world problems. By generating diverse and verifiable environments, SandMLE enables faster execution times, achieving over 13 times improvement compared to traditional methods. The framework demonstrates substantial performance gains in MLE tasks, enhancing exploration and generalization capabilities of RL agents.

zh.title: SandMLE：高效的机器学习工程环境生成
zh.desc: SandMLE是一个多智能体框架，能够从有限的种子任务生成合成的机器学习工程环境。这种方法通过减少数据集规模和计算成本，同时保持问题的复杂性，促进了高效的在线强化学习。与传统的监督微调方法相比，SandMLE在执行时间上减少了超过13倍，使得在机器学习工程领域进行大规模的在线强化学习成为可能。此外，经过训练的策略在未见过的智能体结构上也能很好地泛化，显示出显著的性能提升。

papers.title: HDP: A Lightweight Cryptographic Protocol for Human Delegation Provenance in Agentic AI Systems
papers.abstract: Abstract  Agentic AI systems increasingly execute consequential actions on behalf of human principals, delegating tasks through multi-step chains of autonomous agents. No existing standard addresses a fundamental accountability gap: verifying that terminal actions in a delegation chain were genuinely authorized by a human principal, through what chain of delegation, and under what scope. This paper presents the Human Delegation Provenance (HDP) protocol, a lightweight token-based scheme that cryptographically captures and verifies human authorization context in multi-agent systems. An HDP token binds a human authorization event to a session, records each agent's delegation action as a signed hop in an append-only chain, and enables any participant to verify the full provenance record using only the issuer's Ed25519 public key and the current session identifier. Verification is fully offline, requiring no registry lookups or third-party trust anchors. We situate HDP within the existing landscape of delegation protocols, identify its distinct design point relative to OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange (RFC 8693), JSON Web Tokens (RFC 7519), UCAN, and the Intent Provenance Protocol (draft-haberkamp-ipp-00), and demonstrate that existing standards fail to address the multi-hop, append-only, human-provenance requirements of agentic systems. HDP has been published as an IETF Internet-Draft (draft-helixar-hdp-agentic-delegation-00) and a reference TypeScript SDK is publicly available.

ru.title: Криптографический аудит цепочек делегирования в агентных системах
ru.desc: В статье предлагается протокол Human Delegation Provenance (HDP) для отслеживания цепочек делегирования полномочий в системах с агентами искусственного интеллекта. HDP использует криптографические токены на основе Ed25519 для фиксации и проверки авторизации человека, регистрируя каждый шаг делегирования как подписанное звено в неизменяемой цепи. Протокол позволяет любому участнику верифицировать всю историю полномочий офлайн, без обращения к реестрам или третьим сторонам. Авторы сравнивают HDP с существующими стандартами OAuth 2.0, JWT и UCAN, доказывая, что они не решают задачу многоуровневого аудита человеческих полномочий в системах агентов.

en.title: Ensuring Accountability in Agentic AI with HDP Protocol
en.desc: This paper introduces the Human Delegation Provenance (HDP) protocol, which addresses the accountability gap in multi-agent systems where tasks are delegated by humans. HDP uses a lightweight, token-based approach to cryptographically verify that actions taken by autonomous agents were authorized by a human, detailing the chain of delegation. Each action is recorded as a signed hop in an append-only chain, allowing for easy verification of the entire authorization process without needing external registries. The authors compare HDP to existing protocols like OAuth 2.0 and JSON Web Tokens, highlighting its unique ability to meet the specific needs of agentic systems.

zh.title: 确保人类授权的多代理系统协议
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为人类委托来源（HDP）协议的轻量级令牌方案，旨在解决多代理系统中人类授权的验证问题。HDP令牌通过加密方式捕捉和验证人类授权上下文，确保每个代理的委托行为都被记录在一个仅追加的链中。该协议允许任何参与者使用发行者的Ed25519公钥和当前会话标识符来离线验证完整的来源记录。与现有的委托协议相比，HDP在多跳、仅追加和人类来源要求方面具有独特的设计优势。

papers.title: The Geometric Alignment Tax: Tokenization vs. Continuous Geometry in Scientific Foundation Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Foundation models in biology and physics suffer from geometric distortion due to discrete categorical bottlenecks, with continuous objectives showing significantly better preservation of system geometry compared to discrete tokenization approaches.  AI-generated summary Foundation models for biology and physics optimize predictive accuracy, but their internal representations systematically fail to preserve the continuous geometry of the systems they model. We identify the root cause: the Geometric Alignment Tax, an intrinsic cost of forcing continuous manifolds through discrete categorical bottlenecks. Controlled ablations on synthetic dynamical systems demonstrate that replacing cross-entropy with a continuous head on an identical encoder reduces geometric distortion by up to 8.5x, while learned codebooks exhibit a non-monotonic double bind where finer quantization worsens geometry despite improving reconstruction. Under continuous objectives, three architectures differ by 1.3x; under discrete tokenization, they diverge by 3,000x. Evaluating 14 biological foundation models with rate-distortion theory and MINE, we identify three failure regimes: Local-Global Decoupling, Representational Compression, and Geometric Vacuity. A controlled experiment confirms that Evo 2's reverse-complement robustness on real DNA reflects conserved sequence composition, not learned symmetry. No model achieves simultaneously low distortion, high mutual information, and global coherence.

ru.title: Непрерывные представления вместо дискретной квантизации для сохранения геометрии биологических систем
ru.desc: В этой работе исследуется проблема геометрических искажений в фундаментальных моделях для биологии и физики, которые возникают из-за использования дискретной категориальной квантизации. Авторы показывают, что непрерывные функции потерь (в отличие от cross-entropy) значительно лучше сохраняют геометрию моделируемых систем, уменьшая искажения в 8,5 раза. Через анализ rate-distortion и взаимной информации выявлены три типа отказов в существующих биологических моделях: несогласованность локальных и глобальных представлений, чрезмерное сжатие информации и потеря геометрической структуры. Исследование демонстрирует, что ни одна из протестированных моделей не может одновременно достичь низкого искажения, высокой взаимной информации и глобальной согласованности.

en.title: Continuous Objectives Overcome Geometric Distortion in Foundation Models
en.desc: This paper discusses the limitations of foundation models in biology and physics, particularly how they struggle with geometric distortion when using discrete categorical bottlenecks. The authors introduce the concept of the Geometric Alignment Tax, which highlights the challenges of maintaining continuous geometry in these models. They demonstrate that using continuous objectives instead of discrete tokenization can significantly reduce geometric distortion, improving model performance. The study also identifies various failure modes in biological models, emphasizing the trade-offs between distortion, mutual information, and coherence in their representations.

zh.title: 优化几何保真度，提升基础模型表现
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了生物学和物理学中的基础模型在处理几何失真时所面临的问题。研究发现，离散的分类瓶颈会导致模型无法有效保留系统的连续几何特性。通过对比连续目标和离散标记化方法，结果显示使用连续目标可以显著减少几何失真。论文还识别了三种失败模式，并提出了改进模型表现的潜在方法。

papers.title: SciLT: Long-Tailed Classification in Scientific Image Domains
papers.abstract: Abstract Scientific long-tailed recognition benefits from a proposed framework that leverages multi-level representations through adaptive feature fusion and dual-supervision learning to achieve balanced performance across all classes.  AI-generated summary Long-tailed recognition has benefited from foundation models and fine-tuning paradigms, yet existing studies and benchmarks are mainly confined to natural image domains, where pre-training and fine-tuning data share similar distributions. In contrast, scientific images exhibit distinct visual characteristics and supervision signals, raising questions about the effectiveness of fine-tuning foundation models in such settings. In this work, we investigate scientific long-tailed recognition under a purely visual and parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) paradigm. Experiments on three scientific benchmarks show that fine-tuning foundation models yields limited gains, and reveal that penultimate-layer features play an important role, particularly for tail classes. Motivated by these findings, we propose SciLT, a framework that exploits multi-level representations through adaptive feature fusion and dual-supervision learning. By jointly leveraging penultimate- and final-layer features, SciLT achieves balanced performance across head and tail classes. Extensive experiments demonstrate that SciLT consistently outperforms existing methods, establishing a strong and practical baseline for scientific long-tailed recognition and providing valuable guidance for adapting foundation models to scientific data with substantial domain shifts.
affiliations: Renmin University of China, Gaoling School of Artificial Intelligence

ru.title: Умная фузия признаков для сбалансированной классификации научных данных
ru.desc: Статья посвящена задаче классификации научных изображений с несбалансированным распределением классов (long-tailed recognition). Авторы показали, что стандартная адаптация фундаментальных моделей к научным данным даёт ограниченные результаты из-за значительного различия в характеристиках изображений. Они предложили метод SciLT, который использует многоуровневые представления из разных слоёв нейронной сети и комбинирует их через адаптивное слияние признаков. Такой подход позволяет достичь сбалансированного качества предсказаний как для классов с большим количеством примеров, так и для редких классов.

en.title: Enhancing Scientific Image Recognition with SciLT Framework
en.desc: This paper introduces SciLT, a framework designed to improve long-tailed recognition in scientific images by using adaptive feature fusion and dual-supervision learning. It highlights the challenges of applying foundation models to scientific data, which often have different visual characteristics compared to natural images. The research shows that fine-tuning these models yields limited improvements, especially for tail classes, but emphasizes the importance of using features from both penultimate and final layers. Through extensive experiments, SciLT demonstrates superior performance across all classes, setting a new standard for recognizing scientific images with long-tailed distributions.

zh.title: 科学长尾识别的新框架
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种名为SciLT的框架，用于科学领域的长尾识别。该框架通过自适应特征融合和双重监督学习，利用多层次表示来实现各类的平衡性能。研究表明，倒数第二层特征在长尾类的识别中起着重要作用，尤其是在科学图像中。实验结果显示，SciLT在多个科学基准测试中优于现有方法，为科学数据的基础模型适应提供了有效的指导。

papers.title: Paper Espresso: From Paper Overload to Research Insight
papers.abstract: Abstract _paper espresso is an open-source platform that uses large language models to automatically discover, summarize, and analyze trending arxiv papers, providing multi-granularity trend analysis and revealing dynamics in ai research landscape.  AI-generated summary The accelerating pace of scientific publishing makes it increasingly difficult for researchers to stay current. We present Paper Espresso, an open-source platform that automatically discovers, summarizes, and analyzes trending arXiv papers. The system uses large language models (LLMs) to generate structured summaries with topical labels and keywords, and provides multi-granularity trend analysis at daily, weekly, and monthly scales through LLM-driven topic consolidation. Over 35 months of continuous deployment, Paper Espresso has processed over 13,300 papers and publicly released all structured metadata, revealing rich dynamics in the AI research landscape: a mid-2025 surge in reinforcement learning for LLM reasoning, non-saturating topic emergence (6,673 unique topics), and a positive correlation between topic novelty and community engagement (2.0x median upvotes for the most novel papers). A live demo is available at https://huggingface.co/spaces/Elfsong/Paper_Espresso.

ru.title: Автоматическое отслеживание трендов в AI-исследованиях через LLM-анализ научных публикаций
ru.desc: Paper Espresso — это открытая платформа, которая использует большие языковые модели для автоматического обнаружения, суммаризации и анализа популярных статей с arXiv. Система генерирует структурированные резюме со смысловыми метками и ключевыми словами, а также проводит анализ трендов на разных временных масштабах — дневном, недельном и месячном. За 35 месяцев работы платформа обработала более 13 300 статей и выявила важные закономерности в ландшафте AI-исследований, включая всплеск интереса к reinforcement learning для рассуждений LLM. Полученные структурированные метаданные открыто распространяются, позволяя сообществу лучше понимать динамику развития научных направлений.

en.title: Stay Ahead in AI Research with Paper Espresso!
en.desc: Paper Espresso is an innovative open-source platform that leverages large language models (LLMs) to automatically discover and summarize trending research papers from arXiv. It provides structured summaries with topical labels and keywords, enabling researchers to quickly grasp the essence of new studies. The platform also offers multi-granularity trend analysis, allowing users to track developments in AI research over daily, weekly, and monthly intervals. With over 13,300 papers processed, it reveals significant trends, such as a surge in reinforcement learning and a correlation between topic novelty and community engagement.

zh.title: 自动化分析，掌握AI研究动态
zh.desc: Paper Espresso 是一个开源平台，利用大型语言模型自动发现、总结和分析 arXiv 上的热门论文。该系统生成结构化摘要，并提供多层次的趋势分析，帮助研究人员跟上快速变化的科学出版节奏。通过对超过 13,300 篇论文的处理，Paper Espresso 揭示了人工智能研究领域的动态变化，包括强化学习的激增和新兴主题的出现。该平台的分析结果显示，主题的新颖性与社区参与度之间存在正相关关系。

papers.title: Cog-DRIFT: Exploration on Adaptively Reformulated Instances Enables Learning from Hard Reasoning Problems
papers.abstract: Abstract Task reformulation and curriculum learning enable reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards to overcome exploration barriers in large language model post-training by transforming complex problems into simpler formats that provide denser learning signals.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (RLVR) has improved the reasoning abilities of LLMs, yet a fundamental limitation remains: models cannot learn from problems that are too difficult to solve under their current policy, as these yield no meaningful reward signal. We propose a simple yet effective solution based on task reformulation. We transform challenging open-ended problems into cognitively simpler variants -- such as multiple-choice and cloze formats -- that preserve the original answer while reducing the effective search space and providing denser learning signals. These reformulations span a spectrum from discriminative to generative tasks, which we exploit to bootstrap learning: models first learn from structured, easier formats, and this knowledge transfers back to improve performance on the original open-ended problems. Building on this insight, we introduce Cog-DRIFT, a framework that constructs reformulated variants and organizes them into an adaptive curriculum based on difficulty. Training progresses from easier to harder formats, enabling the model to learn from problems that previously yielded zero signal under standard RL post-training. Cog-DRIFT not only improves on the originally unsolvable hard problems (absolute +10.11% for Qwen and +8.64% for Llama) but also generalizes well to other held-out datasets. Across 2 models and 6 reasoning benchmarks, our method consistently outperforms standard GRPO and strong guided-exploration baselines. On average, Cog-DRIFT shows +4.72% (Qwen) and +3.23% (Llama) improvements over the second-best baseline. We further show that Cog-DRIFT improves pass@k at test time, and the curriculum improves sample efficiency. Overall, our results highlight task reformulation and curriculum learning as an effective paradigm for overcoming the exploration barrier in LLM post-training.

ru.title: От простого к сложному: реформулировка задач для обучения LLM
ru.desc: В статье предлагается метод преодоления барьера исследования при обучении с подкреплением больших языковых моделей через переформулировку задач и обучение по учебному плану. Авторы преобразуют сложные открытые задачи в более простые форматы (множественный выбор, заполнение пропусков), которые сохраняют исходный ответ, но создают плотнее сигналы вознаграждения. Предложенный фреймворк Cog-DRIFT организует эти переформулировки в адаптивный учебный план, прогрессируя от более простых к более сложным форматам. Метод позволяет моделям получить знания из задач, которые ранее не давали сигнала вознаграждения, и показывает значительные улучшения на нескольких бенчмарках рассуждений.

en.title: Transforming Complexity: Learning Made Easier for AI
en.desc: This paper introduces a method called Cog-DRIFT that enhances reinforcement learning for large language models (LLMs) by reformulating complex tasks into simpler ones. By transforming difficult problems into formats like multiple-choice questions, the model can receive clearer learning signals, which helps it learn more effectively. The approach uses a curriculum learning strategy, where training starts with easier tasks and gradually progresses to harder ones, allowing the model to build knowledge incrementally. The results show significant improvements in solving previously unsolvable problems and better generalization across different datasets.

zh.title: 任务重构与课程学习：突破探索障碍的有效策略
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种基于任务重构和课程学习的方法，旨在通过可验证奖励的强化学习来克服大语言模型后训练中的探索障碍。我们将复杂的问题转化为更简单的格式，如多项选择和填空题，从而提供更密集的学习信号。通过这种方式，模型可以先从结构化的简单格式中学习，然后将这些知识迁移回原始的开放性问题。Cog-DRIFT框架通过适应性课程组织这些重构变体，使训练从简单到困难，显著提高了模型在原本无法解决的问题上的表现。

papers.title: POEMetric: The Last Stanza of Humanity
papers.abstract: Abstract POEMetric evaluates LLM poetry generation across basic, creative, and quality dimensions, revealing significant gaps between human and machine capabilities in poetic expression.  AI-generated summary Large Language Models (LLMs) can compose poetry, but how far are they from human poets? In this paper, we introduce POEMetric, the first comprehensive framework for poetry evaluation, examining 1) basic instruction-following abilities in generating poems according to a certain form and theme, 2) advanced abilities of showing creativity, lexical diversity, and idiosyncrasy, evoking emotional resonance, and using imagery and literary devices, and 3) general appraisal of the overall poem quality and estimation of authorship. We curated a human poem dataset - 203 English poems of 7 fixed forms annotated with meter, rhyme patterns and themes - and experimented with 30 LLMs for poetry generation based on the same forms and themes of the human data, totaling 6,090 LLM poems. Based on POEMetric, we assessed the performance of both human poets and LLMs through rule-based evaluation and LLM-as-a-judge, whose results were validated by human experts. Results show that, though the top model achieved high form accuracy (4.26 out of 5.00, with Gemini-2.5-Pro as a judge; same below) and theme alignment (4.99), all models failed to reach the same level of advanced abilities as human poets, who achieved unparalleled creativity (4.02), idiosyncrasy (3.95), emotional resonance (4.06), and skillful use of imagery (4.49) and literary devices (4.67). Humans also defeated the best-performing LLM in overall poem quality (4.22 vs. 3.20). As such, poetry generation remains a formidable challenge for LLMs. Data and codes are released at https://github.com/Bingru-Li/POEMetric.

ru.title: Машины пишут стихи, но душу им не дать
ru.desc: В статье представлена POEMetric — первая комплексная система оценки поэзии, созданной большими языковыми моделями. Исследователи оценили способность 30 LLM генерировать стихи по различным критериям: соответствие форме, творческость, лексическое разнообразие, эмоциональный резонанс и мастерство использования литературных приёмов. Анализ 6090 сгенерированных моделями стихотворений показал, что хотя LLM хорошо справляются с технической стороной (точность формы и темы), они значительно отстают от человека в креативности и глубине художественного выражения. Результаты подтверждают, что генерация поэзии остаётся сложной задачей для современных нейросетей.

en.title: Bridging the Gap: Evaluating AI Poetry Against Human Creativity
en.desc: The paper introduces POEMetric, a new framework designed to evaluate poetry generated by Large Language Models (LLMs) against human poets. It assesses three key dimensions: basic instruction-following, advanced creative abilities, and overall poem quality. The study reveals that while LLMs can generate poems that follow specific forms and themes, they significantly lag behind human poets in creativity, emotional depth, and the use of literary techniques. The findings highlight the ongoing challenges in achieving human-like poetic expression with AI.

zh.title: 诗歌生成：人类与机器的差距
zh.desc: 本论文介绍了POEMetric，这是一个用于评估大型语言模型（LLM）生成诗歌的综合框架。我们通过分析基本的指令遵循能力、创造力和整体诗歌质量，揭示了人类诗人与机器在诗歌表达上的显著差距。研究结果表明，尽管LLM在形式和主题上表现良好，但在创造性、情感共鸣和文学技巧等高级能力上仍无法与人类诗人相提并论。总的来说，诗歌生成对LLM来说仍然是一个巨大的挑战。

papers.title: Do Audio-Visual Large Language Models Really See and Hear?
papers.abstract: Abstract AVLLMs exhibit modality bias where visual representations dominate over audio cues during multimodal integration, despite audio semantics being present in intermediate layers.  AI-generated summary Audio-Visual Large Language Models (AVLLMs) are emerging as unified interfaces to multimodal perception. We present the first mechanistic interpretability study of AVLLMs, analyzing how audio and visual features evolve and fuse through different layers of an AVLLM to produce the final text outputs. We find that although AVLLMs encode rich audio semantics at intermediate layers, these capabilities largely fail to surface in the final text generation when audio conflicts with vision. Probing analyses show that useful latent audio information is present, but deeper fusion layers disproportionately privilege visual representations that tend to suppress audio cues. We further trace this imbalance to training: the AVLLM's audio behavior strongly matches its vision-language base model, indicating limited additional alignment to audio supervision. Our findings reveal a fundamental modality bias in AVLLMs and provide new mechanistic insights into how multimodal LLMs integrate audio and vision.

ru.title: Зрение доминирует над звуком: проблема модального дисбаланса в аудиовизуальных моделях
ru.desc: В этой работе исследуется механизм работы аудиовизуальных больших языковых моделей (AVLLM) с помощью методов интерпретируемости. Авторы обнаружили, что хотя модели кодируют богатую семантику звука на промежуточных слоях, при конфликте между аудио и видео визуальные представления подавляют аудиосигналы в итоговом выводе текста. Анализ показал, что проблема кроется в процессе обучения: модели переносят поведение от визуально-языковых базовых моделей без достаточной адаптации к аудиосигналам. Исследование выявило фундаментальное смещение в пользу модальности зрения в AVLLMs и предоставило новые механистические insights о том, как мультимодальные LLM интегрируют аудио и видео.

en.title: Unveiling Modality Bias in AVLLMs: Vision Over Audio
en.desc: This paper investigates how Audio-Visual Large Language Models (AVLLMs) process and integrate audio and visual information. It reveals that while AVLLMs capture detailed audio features in their intermediate layers, these features often do not influence the final text outputs when there is a conflict with visual data. The study shows that the model's architecture favors visual representations, leading to a suppression of audio cues during the fusion process. Additionally, the findings suggest that the training of AVLLMs is heavily influenced by their vision-language base models, resulting in a significant modality bias favoring visual over audio information.

zh.title: 音视频模型中的模态偏见
zh.desc: 音视频大型语言模型（AVLLMs）在多模态整合中表现出模态偏见，视觉信息在最终文本生成中占主导地位。尽管在中间层中存在丰富的音频语义，但当音频与视觉信息冲突时，这些音频能力在最终输出中未能有效体现。我们的分析表明，尽管有有用的音频信息存在，但深层融合层过于偏向视觉表示，抑制了音频线索的发挥。研究结果揭示了AVLLMs中的基本模态偏见，并提供了多模态大型语言模型如何整合音频和视觉的新机制见解。

papers.title: Type-Checked Compliance: Deterministic Guardrails for Agentic Financial Systems Using Lean 4 Theorem Proving
papers.abstract: Abstract A formal-verification-based AI guardrail platform uses neural-symbolic models to ensure regulatory compliance in autonomous financial AI systems with cryptographic-level certainty.  AI-generated summary The rapid evolution of autonomous, agentic artificial intelligence within financial services has introduced an existential architectural crisis: large language models (LLMs) are probabilistic, non-deterministic systems operating in domains that demand absolute, mathematically verifiable compliance guarantees. Existing guardrail solutions -- including NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails and Guardrails AI -- rely on probabilistic classifiers and syntactic validators that are fundamentally inadequate for enforcing complex multi-variable regulatory constraints mandated by the SEC, FINRA, and OCC. This paper presents the Lean-Agent Protocol, a formal-verification-based AI guardrail platform that leverages the Aristotle neural-symbolic model developed by Harmonic AI to auto-formalize institutional policies into Lean 4 code. Every proposed agentic action is treated as a mathematical conjecture: execution is permitted if and only if the Lean 4 kernel proves that the action satisfies pre-compiled regulatory axioms. This architecture provides cryptographic-level compliance certainty at microsecond latency, directly satisfying SEC Rule 15c3-5, OCC Bulletin 2011-12, FINRA Rule 3110, and CFPB explainability mandates. A three-phase implementation roadmap from shadow verification through enterprise-scale deployment is provided.
affiliations: Thomas Jefferson High School for Science and Technology

ru.title: Формальная верификация как гарантия регуляторного соответствия финансовых AI-агентов
ru.desc: Статья представляет платформу Lean-Agent Protocol, которая использует нейрально-символические модели для обеспечения регуляторного соответствия в автономных финансовых AI-системах. В отличие от вероятностных классификаторов, эта система автоматически формализует политики в код Lean 4 и проверяет каждое действие агента как математическое утверждение, требуя формального доказательства соответствия нормативным требованиям. Платформа обеспечивает криптографический уровень уверенности в соответствии с требованиями SEC, FINRA и OCC с микросекундной задержкой. Предложена трёхфазная дорожная карта внедрения от теневой верификации к масштабному развёртыванию на предприятиях.

en.title: Ensuring Compliance with Formal Verification in Financial AI
en.desc: This paper introduces a new AI guardrail platform called the Lean-Agent Protocol, which uses formal verification to ensure that autonomous financial AI systems comply with strict regulations. It employs a neural-symbolic model to convert institutional policies into Lean 4 code, allowing for precise compliance checks. Each action taken by the AI is treated as a mathematical conjecture, only allowed if it meets regulatory requirements verified by the Lean 4 kernel. This approach offers high-speed compliance assurance, addressing the limitations of existing probabilistic methods in the financial sector.

zh.title: 确保金融AI合规性的智能护栏
zh.desc: 这篇论文介绍了一种基于形式验证的人工智能护栏平台，旨在确保自主金融AI系统的合规性。该平台使用神经符号模型，将机构政策自动形式化为Lean 4代码。每个代理行为都被视为数学猜想，只有在Lean 4内核证明该行为符合预编译的监管公理时，才允许执行。此架构提供了加密级别的合规性保证，能够满足金融监管机构的要求。

papers.title: Emergent Compositional Communication for Latent World Properties
papers.abstract: Abstract Multi-agent systems with Gumbel-Softmax communication develop compositional representations of physical properties from video features without supervision, demonstrating scalable and causally robust learning of latent variables.  AI-generated summary Can multi-agent communication pressure extract discrete, compositional representations of invisible physical properties from frozen video features? We show that agents communicating through a Gumbel-Softmax bottleneck with iterated learning develop positionally disentangled protocols for latent properties (elasticity, friction, mass ratio) without property labels or supervision on message structure. With 4 agents, 100% of 80 seeds converge to near-perfect compositionality (PosDis=0.999, holdout 98.3%). Controls confirm multi-agent structure -- not bandwidth or temporal coverage -- drives this effect. Causal intervention shows surgical property disruption (~15% drop on targeted property, <3% on others). A controlled backbone comparison reveals that the perceptual prior determines what is communicable: DINOv2 dominates on spatially-visible ramp physics (98.3% vs 95.1%), while V-JEPA 2 dominates on dynamics-only collision physics (87.4% vs 77.7%, d=2.74). Scale-matched (d=3.37) and frame-matched (d=6.53) controls attribute this gap entirely to video-native pretraining. The frozen protocol supports action-conditioned planning (91.5%) with counterfactual velocity reasoning (r=0.780). Validation on Physics 101 real camera footage confirms 85.6% mass-comparison accuracy on unseen objects, temporal dynamics contributing +11.2% beyond static appearance, agent-scaling compositionality replicating at 90% for 4 agents, and causal intervention extending to real video (d=1.87, p=0.022).

ru.title: Агенты учат физику через разговор
ru.desc: Статья исследует, как мультиагентные системы с использованием Gumbel-Softmax коммуникации могут извлекать дискретные, композиционные представления скрытых физических свойств (эластичность, трение, массовое отношение) из видеофичей без какого-либо супервизорного обучения. Агенты развивают протоколы общения через узкое место (bottleneck), которые позволяют им разделять информацию о различных физических свойствах так, чтобы каждое свойство кодировалось независимо. Авторы демонстрируют, что структура мультиагентного взаимодействия критична для такого обучения, и показывают, что причинные вмешательства подтверждают действительное изучение физических свойств. Результаты валидируются на реальных видео и показывают применимость для планирования действий и контрфактического рассуждения.

en.title: Learning Physical Properties Through Multi-Agent Communication
en.desc: This paper explores how multi-agent systems can learn to communicate and represent physical properties from video data without any supervision. Using a technique called Gumbel-Softmax, the agents develop effective communication protocols that allow them to identify properties like elasticity and friction. The study demonstrates that these agents can achieve high levels of compositionality, meaning they can break down complex properties into simpler, understandable parts. Additionally, the research shows that the choice of perceptual prior significantly influences the agents' ability to communicate and understand dynamics in their environment.

zh.title: 多智能体系统的无监督学习与物理属性表示
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了多智能体系统如何通过Gumbel-Softmax通信，从视频特征中无监督地学习物理属性的组合表示。研究表明，智能体在没有标签或消息结构监督的情况下，能够发展出位置解耦的协议来表示潜在属性，如弹性、摩擦和质量比。实验结果显示，使用四个智能体的系统在80个种子中100%收敛到近乎完美的组合性。通过因果干预，研究还发现对特定属性的干扰会显著影响结果，而对其他属性的影响则很小。

papers.title: Locally Confident, Globally Stuck: The Quality-Exploration Dilemma in Diffusion Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Diffusion large language models face a tradeoff between generation quality and reasoning path exploration, which is addressed through a novel sampling method that balances these competing objectives.  AI-generated summary Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) theoretically permit token decoding in arbitrary order, a flexibility that could enable richer exploration of reasoning paths than autoregressive (AR) LLMs. In practice, however, random-order decoding often hurts generation quality. To mitigate this, low-confidence remasking improves single-sample quality (e.g., Pass@1) by prioritizing confident tokens, but it also suppresses exploration and limits multi-sample gains (e.g., Pass@k), creating a fundamental quality--exploration dilemma. In this paper, we provide a unified explanation of this dilemma. We show that low-confidence remasking improves a myopic proxy for quality while provably constraining the entropy of the induced sequence distribution. To overcome this limitation, we characterize the optimal distribution that explicitly balances quality and exploration, and develop a simple Independent Metropolis--Hastings sampler that approximately targets this distribution during decoding. Experiments across a range of reasoning benchmarks including MATH500, AIME24/25, HumanEval, and MBPP show that our approach yields better exploration-quality tradeoff than both random and low-confidence remasking.

ru.title: Балансируя качество и исследование: оптимальное декодирование для диффузионных языковых моделей
ru.desc: В статье рассматриваются диффузионные большие языковые модели, которые могут генерировать токены в произвольном порядке, в отличие от авторегрессивных моделей. Авторы выявляют фундаментальный компромисс между качеством генерации (Pass@1) и разнообразием исследования пространства решений (Pass@k), который возникает при использовании стандартных методов переквантизации низкодоверительных токенов. Они предлагают теоретическую характеризацию оптимального распределения, которое явно балансирует качество и разнообразие, и разработали сэмплер на основе независимого алгоритма Метрополиса-Гастингса. Экспериментальные результаты на задачах математического рассуждения, программирования и олимпиадных задачах подтверждают превосходство предложенного метода над существующими подходами.

en.title: Balancing Quality and Exploration in Diffusion Language Models
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenge faced by diffusion large language models (dLLMs) in balancing generation quality and exploration of reasoning paths. The authors introduce a novel sampling method that improves the quality of generated outputs while allowing for better exploration of reasoning paths compared to traditional autoregressive models. They identify a quality-exploration dilemma caused by low-confidence remasking, which enhances single-sample quality but limits broader exploration. By proposing an Independent Metropolis-Hastings sampler, the paper demonstrates a way to achieve an optimal balance between quality and exploration, leading to improved performance on various reasoning benchmarks.

zh.title: 平衡生成质量与推理探索的创新方法
zh.desc: 本文探讨了扩散大语言模型在生成质量与推理路径探索之间的权衡。研究提出了一种新颖的采样方法，旨在平衡这两种相互竞争的目标。通过低置信度重掩码技术，单样本质量得以提升，但同时抑制了探索能力，导致质量与探索之间的根本矛盾。我们提出了一种独立的梅特罗波利斯-哈斯廷斯采样器，能够在解码过程中近似目标分布，从而改善生成质量与探索的平衡。
